author: @yakelinrodriguez-aguero1075

title: EAS/ALST Review Session with Dr. Alan Singer
body: Yes, exactly! Before you go into the EAS exam, know three different strategies that you can use for the ELL scenario, another strategy for special education learners and one more for diverse students scenario. If you know three strategies well enough, you will be able the answer the questions pertaining to the scenarios without any difficulty.
author: @harjits5599

title: EAS/ALST Review Session with Dr. Alan Singer
body: Honestly thank you so much for this Alan and the person responsible for posting this. Im currently studying for both the EAS and THE ALST now. Hopefully everyone passes, we got this! 60% is all we need =)
author: @sarahsalmay

title: A Day in the Life of a Hofstra Student
body: Im at that time in life where I have to think of my future education and Hofstra is definitely one of top choices. The weather in this video humors me, im a born and raised New Yorker and I get it!
author: @crisavls

title: A Day in the Life of a Hofstra Student
body: Yeahhhhh I like it bro!!! This brings me back so much memories to our freshman year! I wish I had this kind of patience to make such a video like this, I'm jealous as fuck!!!
author: @davidafinzi

title: A Day in the Life of a Hofstra Student
body: I'll be apart of the class of 2021 and I'm so happy I stumbled across this video! Everyone seems so friendly and I absolutely love the snow covered campus!
author: @jilliank2154

title: A Day in the Life of a Hofstra Student
body: I hope to be transferring to hofstra next year, thank you for making this video so I could see what it's like, especially during the winter. If I can ask a question, I was curious what the weekend life is like?
author: @scarletriverfox

title: A Day in the Life of a Hofstra Student
body: Loved the video! I was accepted to Hofstra and am really looking forward to most likely attending in the fall! I don't know if you will see this but I was wondering what my odds are of getting a suite as a freshman! And I was also wondering if it's possible to request not only your roommate but your suitemates as well?
author: @kassidypelletier1450

title: A Day in the Life of a Hofstra Student
body:
I just joined the class of 22, and I submitted my housing app. My top choice is Netherlands south. Do you think you can tell me how the walk is to the major academic buildings and if the suits are nice?
Thank you for this video, I can’t make it the admitted students days, so this video was super helpful in reminding me of what the campus is like. Also A+ editing!
author: @ceciliagray6862

title: A Day in the Life of a Hofstra Student
body: hey man I'm from Europe and planning on joining the exchange program for one semester at Hofstra. I have a couple of questions about the life around campus though, could I ask you a couple of things? thanks
author: @RVRx

title: A Day in the Life of a Hofstra Student
body: Hey man, Hofstra is my number one pick coming into this admissions season. Couple of questions because you seem pretty knowledgable! First, in your opinion, how is Hofstra? Are the academics top-notch? How are the dorms? Would you say its proximity to the city has advantages for internships and other opportunities? If I am dead-set on going to Hofstra but it is a little expensive, how generous are they? And finally, when are you going to make another one of these awesome videos? Thanks a ton man, this video was great!
author: @tylerfriedman1245

title: DAY IN A LIFE ~HOFSTRA FRESHMAN 2020~
body: Hi more college videos would be great!! Like can you do ones talking about your experience in New York, like did you bring a car, and since Hofstra isn't directly in New York, NY is the traffic still bad. What is the public transportation like? What kind of food is available on campus? And if you don't mind could you talk about costs? Like what is the average tuition, housing costs, fees, etc?
author: @jordanransom4519

title: DAY IN A LIFE ~HOFSTRA FRESHMAN 2020~
body: can you talk about hofstra in winter? Is it too cold and do you have to stay in the tunnels/indoors for the most part?
